args.query: SELECT "Index", COUNT(*) AS n FROM index_trade GROUP BY "Index" ORDER BY n DESC;

exec_args.sql: SELECT "Index", COUNT(*) AS n FROM index_trade GROUP BY "Index" ORDER BY n DESC;

args.query: SELECT "Index", SUM(CASE WHEN Close>Open THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS up_days, SUM(CASE WHEN Close<Open THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS down_days FROM index_trade WHERE Date >= '2018-01-01' AND Date < '2019-01-01' AND "Index" IN ('NYA','IXIC','GSPTSE') GROUP BY "Index";

exec_args.sql: SELECT "Index", SUM(CASE WHEN Close>Open THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS up_days, SUM(CASE WHEN Close<Open THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS down_days FROM index_trade WHERE Date >= '2018-01-01' AND Date < '2019-01-01' AND "Index" IN ('NYA','IXIC','GSPTSE') GROUP BY "Index";

result.preview: "Indices in North America with more up days than down days in 2018:\n- IXIC (NASDAQ Composite): 44 up days vs 31 down days\n- GSPTSE (S&P/TSX Composite, Toronto): 44 up days vs 41 down days"

args.answer:
Indices in North America with more up days than down days in 2018:
- IXIC (NASDAQ Composite): 44 up days vs 31 down days
- GSPTSE (S&P/TSX Composite, Toronto): 44 up days vs 41 down days
